corrections.description: Contains Pointing Spires poem which should be separate
corrections.text: In the name of Jesus Christ, Amen.

corrections.description: Contains only author name fragment, missing poem text
corrections.text: POINTING SPIRES Symbols of a humble faith; tapered spires Point above the lacy trees in a way To hold my eyes, and warm my heart with fires Of hope that make me want to kneel and pray. | have seen them shine above a loose cloud Rolling in like fog rising from the sod Where people live and toil, speaking aloud Their gratitude while worshipping their God. Firm pointing fills an elemental need Impelling me to lift my voice in praise. Without these symbols others may mislead Me as | grope in darkness for the ways To find a special place with doors ajar Where newer light awaits. There spires are. Pearle M. Olsen

corrections.description: Contains only author name fragment, missing poem text
corrections.text: JUST SO Just so, he Stacked the whisper and the rustle Of the Parchment corn, Heaping hubbard, and banana and the Pumpkin at its side. And so, the blue mountain wore its Thin turban of snow And the sky was A turmoil of departing glory. All remain, repeating the eternals Of the ending year. Now to other hands Their yield to another hoe. Dorothy J. Roberts

corrections.description: Missing the actual poem text, only has content from following article
corrections.text: FOR HIS TOMORROWS His own boat, a cardboard box. His playroom is a deep blue sea. He catches fish (his own small socks). My best broom is a tall palm tree. But now, 'I'll help you, Mommy I'll clean the house and dust it, see!' I wouldn't have him know for worlds How much his help is hindering me. Wilma

corrections.description: Missing the actual poem text, only has content from following article
corrections.text: SPLENDOR IN THE WEST The radiant sun is setting Even as my years go by, And patterns of departing Repeat a promise on the sky. For as the hills await the dawn I see the covenant of light And wait renewal of the morning Beyond the darkness and the night.

missing_articles.title: The Ending Song, Evelyn H. Hughes

notes: Many articles have content bleeding issues, particularly Christmas decoration articles that appear to be merged together. Several short articles like Reminiscing and Silent Music appear to have OCR gaps. The poetry extractions have significant issues with missing content or wrong content. The lesson articles are generally better extracted but have boundary issues.
